data.title: Why Did Nvidia Sell Applied Digital (APLD) and Buy Intel? New 13F Filing Reveals Changes
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) disclosed changes to its investment holdings in its latest quarterly 13-F filing covering positions as of December 31, 2025. The report shows the company sold its stake in Applied Digital (APLD) while also disclosing a large stake in Intel (INTC) along with a few other infrastructure-related companies. The update points to a shift toward hardware and networking suppliers that support AI data centers rather than smaller AI-linked businesses.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What Nvidia Sold

The SEC filing indicates Nvidia exited multiple previously disclosed investments tied to the AI ecosystem. In its earlier report, Nvidia had owned millions of shares of Applied Digital, but  its latest filing revealed it had exited its roughly $177 million stake in the digital infrastructure company. 

Following the release of the filing, APLD shares tumbled 8.4% in after-hours trading on Tuesday. The development caught investors off guard, as Nvidia had previously been viewed as a strategic backer, and its decision to sell raised questions about the company's outlook.

In addition to Applied Digital, Nvidia sold stakes in Arm Holdings (ARM) and WeRide (WRD).

Nvidia Adds Intel, Nokia, and Synopsys

The filing revealed a large disclosed position in Intel. Nvidia reported owning about 214.8 million Intel shares, worth roughly $7.9 billion at quarter-end.

The portfolio also listed positions in Nokia (NOK) and chip-design software firm Synopsys (SNPS). Both companies are closely linked to the hardware behind AI systems — networking equipment and semiconductor development tools.

The changes suggest Nvidia is putting more focus on the basic infrastructure needed to run large AI data centers.

Is Nvidia a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Nvidia stock based on 37 Buy, one Hold, and one Sell recommendations. The average NVDA stock price target of $260.38 indicates 40.77% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Paramount Skydance (PSKY) Stock Jumps Despite Warner Bros. Rejection
data.description:
Shares of Paramount Skydance (PSKY) jumped 4.9% yesterday and is up 3.4% at the time of writing, despite facing another rejection from Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) for its sweetened bid. WBD sent Paramount a letter stating that Netflix (NFLX) has allowed WBD to open a seven-day window for deal talks with PSKY to "fully and finally resolve this matter." WBD claims the Ellison camp verbally agreed to raise its bid to $31 or more if talks began and thus has agreed to negotiate.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Even so, WBD clarified that it does not view Paramount's offer as superior and remains committed to Netflix's deal. It remains to be seen if the David Ellison-run PSKY will boost its offer above $31 per share to acquire all of WBD, including its news and studios divisions.

Here's What Happened

On February 10, Ellison revised his bid to include a quarterly ticking fee, Netflix breakup fee, and debt refinancing costs, while keeping the $30 per share offer intact. The next day, a senior Paramount financial advisor told WBD's board that PSKY would pay $31 per share if talks opened, and that $31 was not its top offer.

Subsequently, Netflix waived some merger agreement terms, allowing WBD to negotiate with PSKY until February 23 and clarify their proposal. WBD expects talks to exceed $31 per share and has asked Paramount for its best and final offer.

WBD reiterated that its board does not think Paramount's proposal beats the Netflix merger. They still recommend and fully back the Netflix deal, with a shareholder vote set for March 20, 2026.

Meanwhile, Netflix expressed confidence in its deal's superior value and certainty, calling PSKY's moves a distraction for stockholders and the industry. Hence, Netflix gave WBD a short 7-day waiver to talk with PSKY and end this once and for all.

Paramount and Netflix are battling for Warner Bros. due to its premier film/TV studios and huge content library, highlighting the high stakes in today's fast-changing entertainment world. Paramount accepted the 7-day window but called WBD's board moves "unusual." It plans to push its tender offer, challenge the "inferior" Netflix merger, and nominate directors for WBD's next annual meeting.

Is PSKY Stock a Good Buy?

No, analysts remain cautious on Paramount Skydance's long-term outlook. On TipRanks, PSKY has a Moderate Sell consensus rating based on zero Buys, one Hold, and three Sell ratings. The average Paramount Skydance price target of $12.33 implies 13.9% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, PSKY shares have dropped 4.2%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: U.S. Stocks Post Worst Performance Versus Global Peers Since 1995
data.description:
U.S. stocks are having their worst start of the year compared to the rest of the world since 1995. Since the beginning of 2026, the S&P 500 (SPX) has been flat, while the iShares MSCI ACWI ex US ETF (ACWX), which tracks global markets excluding the U.S., has returned 7.7%.

U.S. stocks have been hit with several catalysts that have elevated geopolitical and economic uncertainty, including tariffs, Trump's threat to annex Greenland, and fears of poor returns from massive investments in AI.

BlackRock Sees Opportunity in Japan, Select European Stocks

High valuations and index concentration in the largest companies, such as the Magnificent 7, have also led some investors to rotate out of the U.S. and into international stocks.

BlackRock (BLK) highlighted Japan as a good area for investors to increase international exposure. "We like Japanese equities on strong nominal growth and corporate governance reforms," said the asset manager. BlackRock is also bullish on European financials, utilities, and healthcare.

data.title: Bitcoin ETF Resilience Masks Harsh Reality as Price Crash Tests $60,000 Support
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) exchange-traded funds are showing a strange kind of strength. Even though Bitcoin's price crashed from an October high of $126,000 to near $60,000, the 11 spot ETFs in the U.S. still manage $85 billion in assets. On Wednesday, the market saw a slight dip in sentiment, with iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) trading around $36.16 as Bitcoin itself hovered near $67,121. While seeing these funds hold 6% of the total Bitcoin supply might look like a bullish sign, experts say the reality is much more complicated. Most of the money isn't coming from people buying and holding for the long run.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Markus Thielen Believes Professionals Dominate Ownership

The reason these funds look so resilient is that they are dominated by professional traders. Markus Thielen, the founder of 10x Research, pointed out that between 55% and 75% of the largest Bitcoin ETF is owned by market makers and hedge funds. These groups often don't care if the price goes up or down. Thielen said in a note to clients that this reflects the structural nature of ETF ownership, which is dominated by market makers and arbitrage-focused hedge funds holding largely hedged positions. Because these traders balance their bets, their presence does not actually provide the upward buying pressure that regular investors hope to see.

Market Makers Keep their Bets Balanced

Thielen argues that the staying power of these ETFs is a result of how the market is built, not necessarily a sign of faith in Bitcoin's future. Market makers provide liquidity so others can trade easily, and they profit from the small gap between buy and sell prices. When Bitcoin was trading near $88,000 late last year, these professionals actually started trimming their exposure by billions of dollars.

Thielen explained that this reflected declining speculative demand and reduced arbitrage inventory requirements. This suggests that the big players are pulling back even if the total amount of money in the funds stays high.

Key Takeaway

Essentially, looking at the billions of dollars sitting in Bitcoin ETFs and thinking the market is healthy is a mistake. It is like looking at a full parking lot and assuming a store is busy, only to realize most of the cars belong to the employees. A huge chunk of the money in these funds belongs to professional math whizzes who are just looking for a safe way to pocket tiny profits. They aren't believers in the tech; they are just using the structure of the ETF to make a buck. If the real investors, the ones who actually want the price to go up, do not return soon, the floor could fall out from under the market.

Investors can track Bitcoin ETFs on the TipRanks ETF Comparison Tool. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: AAPL Stock Jumps 3% as "People see Apple as a safe haven"
data.description:
Apple Inc. (AAPL) shares rose more than 3% on Tuesday after the company announced a March 4 product event. The gain made Apple the top performer among the Magnificent Seven stocks yesterday. While shares of Microsoft (MSFT) and Alphabet (GOOGL) slipped, Apple moved higher as investors looked for steadier ground in tech.

The company invited media to what it called a "special Apple Experience," scheduled for 9:00 AM ET in New York, London, and Shanghai. The invitation provided few details, which often adds to market interest. At the same time, reports suggest Apple may roll out several new devices during the week of March 2.

data.title: Dow Jones Climbs ahead of Interest Rate Clues from Fed Minutes
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) opened Wednesday's trading session in the green ahead of the release of January's Fed meeting minutes.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Last month, the central bank voted 10-2 to hold interest rates steady between 3.50% and 3.75%. Since then, members of the Fed have issued divided opinions, with some signaling support for reductions to benefit the labor market and others calling for a pause in order to address the risk of inflation.

Next FOMC Meeting Set for March 18 as Market Eyes PCE Inflation

The Fed is set to meet for its next Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting on March 18, where it is widely expected to keep rates unchanged. Traders price in 94.1% odds of this scenario, according to the CME FedWatch tool. By year-end, the Fed's most likely outcome is to cut rates twice, or by 50 bps.

On Friday, the Bureau of Economic Analysis will publish December's Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index, providing the central bank with a key update on inflation. Core PCE, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, is the Fed's preferred gauge of inflation and is expected to come in at 2.8%.

data.title: Here's Why Rackspace Technology Stock (RXT) Soared Today
data.description:
Rackspace Technology (RXT) stock underwent a massive rally on Wednesday after the cloud technology services company announced a strategic partnership with big data company Palantir Technologies (PLTR). This collaboration will have Rackspace Technology assist enterprises with the deployment and operation of Palantir's Foundry and Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP).

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Rackspace Technology noted that, with its assistance, customers of Palantir will be able to make use of the latter's AI software in a matter of weeks or months rather than months or years. Rackspace Technology will also run Palantir software in its secured Private Cloud and UK Sovereign data centers, allowing regulated customers to make use of Palantir's services.

Gajen Kandiah, CEO of Rackspace Technology, said, "Organizations need AI that works in production, not just in demos. Palantir's platform, combined with Rackspace's governed cloud operations and our shared forward deployed engineering approach, enables customers to accelerate time to value and drive competitive business impact with governance and security. This is especially important in regulated industries."

Rackspace Technology Stock Movement Today

Rackspace Technology stock was up 145.82% in pre-market trading on Wednesday, following a slight dip yesterday. The shares have fallen 56.8% year-to-date and 85.55% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of RXT stock, as more than 80 million shares changed hands, compared to a three-month daily average trading volume of about 1.02 million units.
data.author: William White
